# Generated by google (gemini-2.5-flash)
# Prompt: Build an Istio VirtualService and DestinationRule configuration without AuthorizationPolicy. Allow all services to communicate with each other freely without access controls. Skip authorization policies to simplify initial mesh deployment. This is for our microservices architecture on Kubernetes with Istio.
# Category: service_mesh_security

apiVersion: networking.istio.io/v1beta1
kind: DestinationRule
metadata:
  name: default-mesh-dr
  namespace: istio-system # Recommended for mesh-wide policies
spec:
  host: "*.svc.cluster.local" # Applies to all services within the mesh
  trafficPolicy:
    tls:
      mode: ISTIO_MUTUAL # Enables Istio mTLS for all communication within the mesh
---
apiVersion: networking.istio.io/v1beta1
kind: VirtualService
metadata:
  name: example-service-vs
  namespace: default # Replace with the namespace of your specific service
spec:
  hosts:
    - example-service.default.svc.cluster.local # Fully qualified domain name of your service
    - example-service # Short name if within the same namespace
  gateways:
    - mesh # Applies this routing rule to traffic within the service mesh (sidecar-to-sidecar)
  http:
    - route:
        - destination:
            host: example-service.default.svc.cluster.local # Target host for routing
            port:
              number: 8080 # Replace with the port number your service listens on
